jobs.title: Product Manager Market Platform
jobs.jd_full:
About Voltus

Voltus is the leading DER technology platform and virtual power plant operator, connecting distributed energy resources to electricity markets to deliver less expensive, more reliable, and more sustainable electricity. We hire teammates who are bright, gritty, and good, with a genuine passion for a cleaner energy future.

 

The Role

As a Product Manager on the Market Platform pillar, you will own and drive product outcomes that determine how customers participate in electricity markets across all our markets. From how dispatch signals are received and acted on, to how offers are submitted and optimized, to how performance is calculated and settled â you are the connective tissue between customer needs, energy market complexity and the engineering team that builds the systems that maximize financial return and operate to ensure we meet our commitments to markets, customers and Voltans.


Voltus is a fully distributed company and this job is remote. You have the flexibility to work from anywhere, but your workday must align with a US-based time zone.

\n


Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and prioritize outcomes within the Market Platform pillar that will drive the most impact on business objectives and communicate results with stakeholders across the organization

  • Collaborate with engineering manager(s) to align on product requirements and manage the day-to-day efforts of ongoing initiatives and workstreams

  • Formulate investment theses grounded in user needs, market mechanics, and business objectives

  • Own the product roadmap,vision, success criteria and metrics for your product area

  • Effectively validate hypotheses and assumptions about market mechanics, user needs, and system behavior

  • Ensure product solutions are operationally viable, adequately supported, and aligned with business and regulatory strategy


Qualifications

  • At least 2-3 years of experience as a product manager or a role building products

  • Exceptionally strong written and verbal communication skills

  • Strong technical acumen â comfortable reviewing specs and engaging deeply with engineers

  • Self-starter with the ability to manage and prioritize multiple work streams simultaneously

  • Ability to distill qualitative and quantitative information into actionable product decisions

  • Bonus: Experience in energy markets, market operations, or energy technology


\n
$120,000 - $135,000 a year
Base pay is $120k - $135k annually, commensurate with experience, with 10% annual bonus, and equity. Voltus also offers a range of benefits including: unlimited leave for full-time employees, parental leave, and a comprehensive benefits package to promote health, wellness, and financial security.
\n

Please note that at this time, we do not sponsor visas or transfers. Voltus teammates need to be authorized to work from their home location (in the US or Canada, unless otherwise indicated on the role description).

 

Additionally, while Voltus is an all-remote workplace, we have limitations on where employees are able to work for regulatory and security reasons. We expect that Voltans are working primarily from their home country. Working while traveling to other countries must be approved as per our Global Remote Travel Policy.

 

At Voltus, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer because we recognize that a diverse organization begins with a diverse candidate pool. This means we do not tolerate discrimination of any kind and are committed to providing equal employment opportunities regardless of your gender identity, race, nationality, religion, age, sexual orientation, veteran status, disability status, or marital status.



Please mention the word **SLEEK** and tag RMTcyLjE4Mi4xOTMuMTI4 when applying to show you read the job post completely (#RMTcyLjE4Mi4xOTMuMTI4). This is a beta feature to avoid spam applicants. Companies can search these words to find applicants that read this and see they're human.

jobs.title: Technical Project Manager
jobs.jd_full:
We’re ALTEN Technology USA, an engineering company helping clients bring groundbreaking ideas to life—from advancing space exploration and life-saving medical devices to building autonomous electric vehicles. With 3,000+ experts across North America, we partner with leading companies in aerospace, medical devices, robotics, automotive, commercial vehicles, EVs, rail, and more.

As part of the global ALTEN Group—57,000+ engineers in 30 countries—we deliver across the entire product development cycle, from consulting to full project outsourcing.

When you join ALTEN Technology USA, you’ll collaborate on some of the world’s toughest engineering challenges, supported by mentorship, career growth opportunities, and comprehensive benefits. We take pride in fostering a culture where employees feel valued, supported, and inspired to grow.

Location: Foster City, CA

We’re looking for a highly self-sufficient engineer who can operate as a hybrid Harness / Mechanical Integration Engineer to enable fast, safe, and serviceable implementation of low-voltage electrical systems in our vehicles. This role bridges harness routing/retention and mechanical packaging, focusing on practical solutions such as brackets, clips, clamps, channels, strain relief, and protective features to ensure electrical components and harnesses can be installed reliably in real vehicles.

The ideal candidate can independently take loosely defined integration problems, drive the design to a manufacturable

solution, and support prototype builds with minimal guidance.

Responsibilities

    • Own the delivery status of specific attribute roadmap milestones. You ensure that “on track” is a reality, not just a status color, by identifying and clearing blockers before they stall progress.

    • Work 1:1 with engineering and test leads to surface local execution risks — such as vehicle downtime, instrumentation delays, or hardware shortages — and implement immediate recovery plans.

    • Run, maintain, and continuously improve our JIRA structures, sprint cadences, and reporting rituals. Your goal is to create a system that allows engineers to focus on engineering while

    • self-coordinating through your framework.

    • Manage and disposition all cross-functional requests, ensuring dependencies between stakeholders are clearly defined and closed.

    • Translate moving targets and technical complexities into concise, actionable updates for leadership.

Qualifications

    • 2+ years of experience in project management and a Bachelor’s in Business, Communications, Engineering, or a related field.

    • Experience with resource management software (e.g., JIRA) to develop project plans and track asset availability against timelines.

    • Successful track record of delivering multiple complex automotive technical projects in parallel.

    • High degree of understanding of vehicle testing and development. You should understand system interactions and dependencies.

    • Familiarity with prototype or pre-production vehicle testing and development.

    • Expert-level ability to structure boards, workflows, and dashboards that provide real-time visibility into project health without creating “admin debt” for engineers.

    • A “closer” mentality with a high sense of ownership. You are known for closing loops without supervision and preventing scope creep in fast-moving environments.

    • Calm under pressure, concise in communication, and capable of synthesizing shifting priorities into a clear path forward.

Bonus Qualifications

    • Background in Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or a related field.

    • Experience in a startup or high-growth “execution-first” environment.

    • Familiarity with vehicle development cycles and prototype hardware management.

    • Experience with autonomous vehicle platforms and software development.

Salary Range

    • $110,000-120,000

    • The actual salary offered is dependent on various factors including, but not limited to, location, the candidate’s combination of job-related knowledge, qualifications, skills, education, training, and experience

ALTEN Technology is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Our Policy is to extend opportunities to qualified applicants and employees on an equal basis regardless of an individual’s age, race, color, sex, religion, national origin, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression or veteran status.

Please beware of job seeker scams and see this important notice on our careers page for more information about our recruiting process.

Compliance Notice: Alten USA is a federal contractor subject to the requirements of the Vietnam Era Veterans’ Readjustment Assistance Act (VEVRAA) and Executive Order 11246. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and consider all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or veteran status.

Drug Screening Requirement: As a federal contractor, Alten USA maintains a drug-free workplace. All candidates selected for employment will be required to successfully complete a pre-employment drug screening as a condition of hire.

jobs.title: Product Manager Agentic AI
jobs.jd_full:
Job Title: Agentic AI Product Manager

Location: Remote/USA

Reports to: Agentic AI COE Leader

Position Overview:

We are seeking a highly motivated Agentic AI Product Manager to drive the market launch and IP roadmap of our innovative AI-powered solutions collaborating with sales, marketing, consulting and development. This role will work closely with the Agentic AI COE Leader to transform cutting-edge technologies—including autonomous agents, LLMs, and multi-agent systems—into scalable, customer-centric products integrated with platforms like ServiceNow.

The ideal candidate will have strong experience in AI product lifecycle management, cross-functional collaboration, and aligning technology capabilities with business goals.

Key Responsibilities:

Product Strategy & Market Analysis

- Understand competitive market intelligence and conduct research to inform our AI roadmap and features while ensuring product/market fit.

- Define product requirements and roadmaps in partnership with the Agentic AI COE Leader and cross-functional teams that results in customer impact (cost reduction, time savings, strategic differentiation, etc.)

- Support the COE Leader in assessing AI innovation opportunities across industries. And translate strategic AI innovation goals into tangible product development initiatives.

- Identify use cases where agentic AI can enhance and/or orchestrate enterprise workflows, particularly on ServiceNow.

- Develop ROI models and frameworks for realizing value of our AI offerings and solutions

Cross-functional Collaboration

- Collaborate with sales, solution consulting, design, engineering, data science, and ServiceNow platform teams to deliver high-impact AI capabilities and IP

- Work closely with client-facing teams to identify POC’s opportunities, prioritize features, gather feedback and ensure adoption (i.e. production rollout) success.

- Work with our engineering teams to facilitate the agile development processes, define epics, elaborate user stories, translate and prioritize backlog and conduct retrospectives

AI IP Development

- Manage product lifecycle from ideation to launch, with a strong emphasis on usability, performance, ROI and business outcomes.

- Ensure products integrate seamlessly with ServiceNow (e.g., ITSM, HRSD, SecOps).

- Oversee feature validation, testing, and iteration using agile methods and customer feedback.

Market Awareness & Launch

- Stay informed on emerging trends in agentic AI, LLMs, multi-agent systems, and enterprise automation.

- Evaluate and prioritize experimental ideas, proof-of-concepts, and beta features with our clients and partners.

- Participate and orchestrate market awareness events like webinars, client roundtables, analyst presentations, partner briefings, etc.

- Assist in developing product marketing collateral such as data sheets, product positioning, FAQ’s, sales enablement, etc.

- Ensure product success in market measured by IP and services revenue from AI products and the number of production go-lives

Qualifications:

- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Business, Engineering, or a related field.

- 3+ years of experience in product management with a focus on AI, machine learning, or enterprise software.

- 7+ years of experience in a consulting or a product company.

- Experience integrating AI solutions into platforms like ServiceNow or similar enterprise systems.

- Strong understanding of AI/ML concepts including LLMs, autonomous agents, and NLP.

- Experience with AI cloud services from AWS, Google GCP, Azure, etc.

- Experience in launching enterprise AI solutions with exposure to AI governance, ethics, responsibility, etc.

- Experience working in services or consulting environments preferred specifically with AI

Preferred Skills:

- Familiarity with ServiceNow’s AI features (e.g., Virtual Agent, Predictive Intelligence).

- Data Science and Python experience

- Understanding of MLOps lifecycle and human-in-the-loop design.

- Strong storytelling, prioritization, and stakeholder communication skills.

- Industry knowledge in sectors such as BFSI and Telecom, is a plus.

jobs.title: Product Manager, Data & Insights
jobs.jd_full:
Product Manager — Data & Fleet Insights Platform

Department: RB Marketplace

About the Role

Rouse, an RB Global Marketplace brand, is seeking a Product Manager to lead the evolution of a data-driven platform powering fleet management and full-service asset disposition. This role sits at the intersection of data architecture, product strategy, and commercial impact—owning how we translate complex datasets into scalable, high-leverage product experiences.

You will define and drive product initiatives that shape how enterprise clients manage, value, and disposition their assets across global markets. This is a highly strategic role with direct influence on business outcomes, requiring strong product judgment, technical fluency, and the ability to operate autonomously in ambiguous problem spaces.

What You’ll Own

  • Define and execute product strategy across a portfolio of data-intensive products and capabilities

  • Architect scalable product solutions by partnering closely with engineering and data teams to ensure data models support long-term product extensibility

  • Evaluate existing systems and infrastructure to identify constraints, inefficiencies, and opportunities for modernization aligned with future roadmap objectives

  • Lead cross-functional initiatives spanning product, engineering, data science, commercial, and operations teams

  • Translate business goals into clear product direction, ensuring alignment across stakeholders and functions

  • Develop product narratives that connect vision, roadmap, and execution across diverse audiences (executive, technical, commercial)

  • Drive discovery and problem framing rooted in a deep understanding of user workflows, client needs, and market dynamics

  • Own prioritization decisions and trade-offs, balancing immediate delivery with long-term platform scalability

  • Deliver measurable outcomes tied to revenue growth, operational efficiency, and client experience

What We’re Looking For

Core Capabilities

  • Strong product intuition with the ability to identify and frame high-impact problems in complex, data-rich environments

  • Proven ability to design and evolve data structures that scale product functionality over time

  • Deep understanding of how to assess existing architecture and determine when to iterate vs. rebuild

  • Exceptional problem-solving skills, with the ability to navigate ambiguity and uncover non-obvious insights

  • Demonstrated experience leading cross-functional initiatives with multiple stakeholders and competing priorities

  • Strong communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to articulate a clear product vision across organizational levels

  • Ability to operate independently, with high ownership and accountability for outcomes

Technical & Analytical Fluency

  • Experience working with data platforms, analytics products, or data-driven decision systems

  • Ability to collaborate effectively with engineering and data teams on system design, data modeling, and platform constraints

  • Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into actionable product decisions

  • Familiarity with modern data ecosystems (e.g., data pipelines, APIs, warehouse architectures)

Product Leadership

  • Track record of building or transforming products into scalable, high-growth business drivers

  • Ability to align product strategy with broader business objectives while maintaining a strong user-centric perspective

  • Experience influencing senior stakeholders and driving alignment across complex organizational structures

  • High standards for execution quality, clarity, and operational rigor

Baseline Qualifications

  • 3+ years of product management experience, preferably in B2B, SaaS, or data-driven platforms

  • Experience working in or alongside data analytics, marketplace, or operational platforms

  • Proven ability to deliver complex products from concept through launch and iteration

Nice-to-Have (Market Expectations)

  • Experience in fleet management, logistics, heavy equipment, or asset lifecycle industries

  • Exposure to pricing, valuation, or disposition workflows

  • Familiarity with global markets and multi-region product considerations

  • Experience working in high-growth or transformation-stage organizations

  • Background in SQL, data analysis, or technical disciplines

How You Work

  • You think in systems, not features

  • You prioritize outcomes over outputs

  • You are comfortable making decisions with incomplete information

  • You bring structure to ambiguity and clarity to complexity

  • You elevate the people and teams around you

jobs.title: Project Manager
jobs.jd_full:
The Role

As a Project Manager, you support the planning and execution of integrated marketing campaigns across strategy, creative, and media.

In this role, you operate at the center of cross-functional teams, partnering with Client Experience and internal disciplines to move work from idea to execution. You manage timelines, coordinate workflows, and support delivery—keeping projects on track, on budget, and at a high standard.

You bring structure to complexity, support collaboration across teams, and focus on the details that keep work moving forward. As you grow in the role, you build your ability to navigate ambiguity, strengthen your project management skills, and contribute to delivering high-quality work.

You Will

  • Own project execution end to end—managing timelines, budgets, deliverables, and communication with precision and accountability
  • Build a strong understanding of client brands, goals, and the healthcare landscape to better support the work
  • Translate client briefs and strategic direction into clear, actionable project plans
  • Coordinate across strategy, creative, media, production, and development teams to keep work aligned and moving forward
  • Track timelines, deliverables, and milestones—proactively communicating progress and flagging risks early
  • Identify and help resolve resource, timing, or scope challenges to maintain momentum
  • Develop and maintain detailed project plans, status trackers, and documentation
  • Monitor tasks, dependencies, and approvals to ensure deadlines are met without sacrificing quality
  • Support budget tracking, including vendor coordination, time tracking, and invoicing processes
  • Contribute to budget updates, reconciliations, and reporting
  • Uphold project management best practices, ensuring consistency and organization across all work streams
  • Maintain clear, organized documentation across briefs, assets, revisions, and communications

You Have

  • 2–4 years of project management experience, preferably within a marketing or advertising agency
  • Familiarity with integrated marketing campaigns and digital/web projects
  • Knowledge of project management methodologies (Waterfall and Agile)
  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality
  • Collaborative, reliable team player
  • Excellent communication and organizational skills
  • Detail-oriented, collaborative, and proactive mindset
  • Experience in healthcare marketing is a plus, but not required

Perks and Benefits

  • Competitive Salary
  • Health, Dental and Vision benefit packages to fit all types of lifestyles
  • Life, Short- and Long-Term Disability Insurance paid 100% by the company
  • FSA / HSA Options
  • 401(K) with a generous company match program
  • Flexible Paid Time Off (aka- unlimited)
  • Paid Parental Leave
  • Hybrid and Remote work schedules available
  • Employee Assistance Program (unlimited free telephonic counseling for a variety of issues such as mental health, financial support, and legal advice)
  • Employee Referral Program

Equality Opportunity Employer Statement Unlock, and its affiliate companies, is an equal opportunity employer dedicated to building an inclusive and diverse workforce. We provide equal employment opportunities without regard to an applicant’s race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, age, national origin, disability status, genetic information, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

About Us:

Unlock Health is a full-service marketing communication agency, grounded in technology, built for healthcare - and integrated with a managed care consultancy with deep revenue strategy expertise. We brought together leading healthcare firms to solve a core challenge: aligning brand, marketing, technology, and revenue strategy so healthcare organizations can attract and retain patients, grow brand equity, and protect their margins. We help our clients do everything from patient acquisition and brand building, to simplifying complex martech ecosystems, and creating digital experiences. Our work is rooted in clarity, authenticity, and a deep understanding of what moves healthcare forward.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Experian is a global data and technology company, powering opportunities for people and businesses around the world. We help to redefine lending practices, uncover and prevent fraud, simplify healthcare, create digital marketing solutions, and gain deeper insights into the automotive market, all using our unique combination of data, analytics and software. We also assist millions of people to realise their financial goals and help them to save time and money.

We operate across a range of markets, from financial services to healthcare, automotive, agrifinance, insurance, and many more industry segments.

We invest in people and new advanced technologies to unlock the power of data and to innovate. A FTSE 100 Index company listed on the London Stock Exchange (EXPN), we have a team of 23,300 people across 32 countries. Our corporate headquarters are in Dublin, Ireland. Learn more at experianplc.com.

Job Description

Experian Software Solutions (ESS) primary objective is dedicated to capitalizing on Experian’s unparalleled data assets through an innovative, market-leading portfolio of world-class software focused on Analytics, Credit Marketing, Decisioning, and Fraud use cases.

We are seeking a collaborative Senior Product Manager to support the expansion of our Global Analytics product portfolio. You will play a critical part in enabling scalable, efficient model development and AI capabilities across initiatives. You will work with your peers and other cross-functional teams across the globe to deliver impactful solutions that support data science workflows and enterprise-wide innovation.

You will report to the Product Management Senior Director.

You’ll have the opportunity to:

  • Define and execute long-term product roadmap, aligning with global strategy, for model development tools which include Global Ascend Sandbox, Feature Factory, Experian Assistant, and product management support for Analytical Services.
  • Collaborate with global technology and regional product teams to build and launch Analytics capabilities.
  • Articulate near and long-term strategy and roadmap to senior internal and external stakeholders with the ability to show the bigger picture.
  • Gather input from internal stakeholders and end users to define clear, actionable product requirements for development teams that support scalable model development and analytics enablement.
  • Collaborate with our regions to identify and operationalize key customer value pools, product areas, and geographic regions that offer the greatest potential for impact.
  • Act as a liaison between technical and non-technical teams, ensuring transparency, alignment, and updates on progress, risks, and dependencies.
  • Analyze established key metrics to determine the overall health of the product, mitigate external and internal risks, and utilize insights to bring changes to product roadmaps.
  • Streamline product processes to enhance efficiency, reduce time-to-market, and ensure high-quality standards.
  • Help improve product development processes, documentation standards, and team collaboration practices to drive efficiency and quality.

Qualifications

Your background:

  • 7+ years of Product Management experience, including at least 2 years in a Senior Product Manager role, preferably in data, analytics, or platform-focused environments working through end-to-end product lifecycle.
  • Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field.
  • Strong understanding of model development workflows and data science tools.
  • Experience launching successful products, working with globally distributed teams and managing cross-time zone collaboration.
  • Experience in Agile delivery/development methodologies.

Additional Information

Benefits/Perks:

  • Great compensation package and bonus plan.
  • Core benefits including medical, dental, vision, and matching 401K.
  • Flexible work environment, ability to work remote, hybrid or in-office.
  • Flexible time off including volunteer time off, vacation, sick and 12-paid holidays.
  • Explore all our exciting benefits here: https://yourexperianbenefits.com/cand-index.html.

At Experian, our people and culture set us apart. We’re deeply committed to creating an environment where everyone feels they belong and can excel. From inclusion and authenticity to work/life balance, development, wellness, collaboration, and recognition, we focus on what truly matters. Our people-first approach has earned us global recognition: World’s Best Workplaces™ 2024 (Fortune Top 25), Great Place To Work™ 2025 in 26 countries, and Glassdoor Best Places to Work 2024, among others.

Want to see what life at Experian is really like? Explore Experian Life on social or visit our careers site.

Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets. The base pay range for this position is listed above. Within this range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors such as job-related skills, experience, and education. You will be also eligible for a variable pay opportunity.

Experian is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer for all groups protected under applicable federal, state and local law, including protected veterans and individuals with disabilities. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know at the earliest opportunity.

#LI-Remote

This is a remote position.

jobs.title: Technical Program Manager
jobs.jd_full:
About Us

Our leading SaaS-based Global Employment Platform™ enables clients to expand into over 180 countries quickly and efficiently, without the complexities of establishing local entities. At G-P, we’re dedicated to breaking down barriers to global business and creating opportunities for everyone, everywhere.

Our diverse, remote-first teams are essential to our success. We empower our Dream Team members with flexibility and resources, fostering an environment where innovation thrives and every contribution is valued and celebrated.

The work you do here will positively impact lives around the world. We stand by our promise: Opportunity Made Possible. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, we invite you to join us in expanding your skills and helping to reshape the future of work.

At G-P, we assist organizations in building exceptional global teams in days, not months—streamlining the hiring, onboarding, and management process to unlock growth potential for all.

About The Position:

To serve as the strategic leader of our engineering operating system. You will own the health, configuration, and insights derived from the Jira and Jellyfish tools, ensuring our leadership has a crystal-clear view of engineering investment, velocity, and health across the entire organisation.

We expect this candidate to be an “AI-First” practitioner, using agentic tooling to automate the mundane and focus on high-leverage organizational improvements.

What You Will Do:

  1. Tooling Architecture & Governance
  • Jira Cloud Expert: Design, build and maintain complex workflows, permission schemes, and automation rules at scale.
  • Jellyfish Orchestration: Map Jira “Signals” to Jellyfish allocation buckets. Ensure the data reflects the true “Investment Distribution” (e.g., separating KTLO/Maintenance from Innovation).
  • System Integrity: Standardize issue types and field usage across 20+ squads to ensure Jellyfish insights are statistically valid.
  1. AI-Augmented Operations
  • Natural Language Querying: Use Atlassian Intelligence to translate plain-English requests into complex JQL for instant reporting.
  • AI Impact Analysis: Leverage Jellyfish AI Impact to measure how AI coding assistants (Copilot, Cursor) are actually shifting our “Time to Deliver” and code quality.
  • Workflow Automation: Integrate  AI Agents to handle “ticket hygiene”—automatically flagging stale issues, summarizing long comment threads for stakeholders, and triaging incoming requests.
  • Synthetic Insights: Use LLMs (Claude/ChatGPT) to synthesize monthly Jellyfish data into executive-level narratives that explain the “Why” behind the “What.”
  1. Strategic Delivery Insights
  • DORA & Flow Metrics: Monitor Cycle Time, Lead Time, and Deployment Frequency to identify systemic bottlenecks. Also identify improvement opportunities.
  • Capitalization Reporting: Partner with Finance to use Jellyfish data for R&D tax credit reporting and labor capitalization.
  • Stakeholder Engagement: Work with stakeholders on triaging and aligning requirements in conjunction with tool administrators for a seamless rollout

What We Are Looking For:

Minimum Requirements :

  • Experience:

    • 5+ years in Engineering Operations (EngOps), Program Management, or a similar data-heavy engineering role.

  • Tooling Expertise:

    • Jira Cloud: Expert-level administration skills.
    • Jellyfish: Power user proficiency.

  • Analytics & Metrics:

    • Deep understanding of Agile metrics and Engineering Investment categories.
    • Strong knowledge of DORA metrics (Deployment Frequency, Lead Time for Changes, Change Failure Rate, and Time to Restore Service).

  • Technical Skills:

    • Advanced proficiency in Jira Automation and JQL.
    • Foundational knowledge of the SDLC process, Scrum, and SAFe methodologies.

  • AI Proficiency:

    • Comfortable leveraging AI agents and LLMs for data analysis and scripting tasks.

Preferred Skills & Qualifications

  • Programming & Integration: Ability to write basic Python or JavaScript for API integrations.
  • Cloud & Version Control: Experience with GitHub and AWS is considered a significant bonus.

Actual compensation for this position may vary and will depend on multiple factors such as relevant qualifications, experience, education, and geographic location. For Full-Time Regular Employees, this position is also eligible for additional compensation as follows:

  • Sales Roles: This position is eligible for a commission structure in addition to base salary.
  • Non-Sales Roles: This position is eligible for an annual bonus which is paid dependent on various factors, including and without limitation, individual and company performance in addition to base salary.

Benefits

G-P values its employees and offers excellent benefits and perks including generous paid parental leave, flexible time off, spending accounts, medical insurance, dental insurance, vision insurance, sabbatical after 5 years and more.

Individuals residing, or applying to work, in the United States: California or Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, please review the following additional information:

G-P will consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records in accordance with the California Fair Chance Act, Los Angeles City Fair Chance Act Ordinance, Los Angeles County Fair Chance Act Ordinance, and San Francisco Fair Chance Act Ordinance. Los Angeles applicants can review additional information regarding the Los Angeles City Fair Chance Act here:Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, and Philadelphia applicants can review information pertaining to Philadelphia’s Fair Criminal Record Screening Standards Ordinance here:Fair Chance Poster. Any consideration of a candidate’s background check with arrest or conviction records will include an individualized assessment based on the factors required by applicable law, including the candidate’s specific record and the duties and requirements of the specific job.

G-P. Global Made Possible.

G-P is a proud Equal Opportunity Employer, and we are committed to building and maintaining a diverse, equitable and inclusive culture that celebrates authenticity. We prohibit discrimination and harassment against employees or applicants on the basis of race, color, creed, religion, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, sex or gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, and pregnancy-related conditions), gender identity or expression (including transgender status), sexual orientation, marital status, military service and veteran status, physical or mental disability, genetic information, or any other legally protected status.

G-P also is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. Individuals with disabilities are encouraged to apply for these positions. If you need an accommodation due to a disability during the interview process, please contact us at careers@g-p.com.

jobs.title: Senior Project Manager
jobs.jd_full:
The Role

The Senior Project Manager drives the execution of integrated marketing engagements—bringing strategy, creative, media, and data together into work that is delivered seamlessly and at a high standard.

In this role, you ensure complex programs remain organized, on track, and moving forward by managing timelines, coordinating workflows, and connecting strategy to execution. You focus on the details that matter—ensuring work is delivered as planned, with consistency and quality.

You build strong working relationships across clients and internal teams, developing a clear understanding of brand, business goals, and project needs. Success in this role requires the ability to navigate complexity, bring structure to ambiguity, and take ownership of delivering high-quality work.

You Will

  • Lead integrated project execution across strategy, creative, media, and data—ensuring work is aligned, coordinated, and delivered at a high standard
  • Build deep knowledge of your clients, their brands, and the healthcare landscape to better guide execution and decision-making
  • Partner closely with Client Experience (CX) and cross-functional teams to translate strategic direction into clear, actionable plans
  • Develop and own project plans, timelines, and workflows—ensuring delivery is on time, on scope, and on budget
  • Manage large-scale, complex programs with multiple workstreams, dependencies, and stakeholders
  • Act as a central connector across teams, ensuring alignment and momentum from kickoff through delivery
  • Oversee client financials, including budgets, forecasting, invoicing, and overall financial health of engagements
  • Proactively identify risks, resolve challenges, and adapt plans to keep work moving forward
  • Uphold and evolve project management best practices—driving consistency, efficiency, and quality across engagements
  • Champion the value of project management across the organization, strengthening processes and demonstrating impact at the enterprise level

You Have

  • 5+ years of integrated project management experience; agency experience required
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment and meet tight deadlines
  • Flexible, solutions-oriented approach to managing projects and team workflows
  • Clear and effective communicator with both internal teams and clients
  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality
  • Collaborative, reliable team player

About Us:

Unlock Health is a full-service marketing communication agency, grounded in technology, built for healthcare - and integrated with a managed care consultancy with deep revenue strategy expertise. We brought together leading healthcare firms to solve a core challenge: aligning brand, marketing, technology, and revenue strategy so healthcare organizations can attract and retain patients, grow brand equity, and protect their margins. We help our clients do everything from patient acquisition and brand building, to simplifying complex martech ecosystems, and creating digital experiences. Our work is rooted in clarity, authenticity, and a deep understanding of what moves healthcare forward.
